questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: The United Nations Charter, which serves as the foundational treaty of the organization, was signed in 1945 in which city?
questions.explanation: The UN Charter was signed on June 26, 1945, in San Francisco, at the conclusion of the United Nations Conference on International Organization.
questions.takeaway: The UN was established in 1945 with the signing of its Charter in San Francisco, though its headquarters was later established in New York.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: The UN was established in 1945 with its Charter signed in San Francisco.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: Which of the following is classified as a 'Specialized Agency' of the United Nations rather than one of its 'Main Organs'?
questions.explanation: The Secretariat, ECOSOC, and Trusteeship Council are three of the six 'main organs' of the UN. The International Labour Organization (ILO) is one of the 15 'specialized agencies'.
questions.takeaway: Distinguishing between the six main organs and the specialized agencies is fundamental to understanding the UN structure.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Six main organs: UNGA, UNSC, ECOSOC, ICJ, Trusteeship Council, and Secretariat. 15 Specialized Agencies include FAO, ILO, IMF, UNESCO, WHO, and World Bank.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: Which of the following correctly describes the relationship or distinction between the 'Main Organs' and 'Specialized Agencies' of the United Nations?

options.text: Main organs are established by the UN Charter, while specialized agencies are autonomous organizations linked to the UN through agreements.
All main organs are headquartered in New York, while all specialized agencies are headquartered in Europe.
Membership in the United Nations automatically grants a state membership in all 15 specialized agencies.
Specialized agencies like the IMF and World Bank are considered main organs because of their global financial influence.

questions.explanation: Option A is correct: The six main organs are the core structure defined by the 1945 Charter. Specialized agencies (like WHO, IMF, FAO) are separate, autonomous organizations with their own memberships and HQs. Option B is incorrect as the ICJ (Main Organ) is in The Hague and some specialized agencies (IMF, World Bank) are in Washington, DC. Option C is incorrect as membership in agencies is often separate. Option D is incorrect as IMF/World Bank are specialized agencies, not main organs.
questions.takeaway: Specialized agencies are autonomous and distinct from the six principal organs of the UN.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: It comprises six main organs... It also includes 15 specialized agencies with distinct headquarters... Specialized Agencies include FAO (Rome), ILO (Geneva), IMF (Washington, DC), UNESCO (Paris), WHO (Geneva), and World Bank (Washington, DC).
